papers.title_en: HeadRoom: Lightweight, Edge-deployable Pipeline for Adaptive Notification Routing
papers.authors: Dinithi Dissanayake, Prasanth Sasikumar, Suranga Nanayakkara
papers.evidence: 基于第一人称视频感知负载的自适应通知路由，与基于注视的前瞻性交互范式一致
papers.tldr: 可穿戴设备如智能眼镜可通过多感官通道传递通知，但如何选择合适的时刻和通道尚不明确。本文提出HeadRoom，一种轻量级边缘可部署管道，从第一人称视频和音频实时估计视觉和听觉通道的可用性。用户实验表明，高感知负载下路由至更可用通道可缩短响应时间。该工作为可穿戴和沉浸式系统中的自适应通知路由开辟了新可能性。
papers.abstract_en: Emerging wearables, such as smart glasses, can deliver notifications through multiple sensory channels, but there is still a limited understanding of how to choose the right channel at the right moment. We propose HeadRoom, a lightweight, edge-deployable pipeline that estimates the availability of visual and auditory channels in real time from egocentric video and audio. Our controlled user study (N=25) shows that, under high perceptual load, routing notifications to the more available channel reduces response time relative to routing them to the less available channel. This work opens up a new possibility for adaptive routing of notifications in wearable and immersive systems.

papers.title_en: Spontaneous eye blinks as temporal markers of internal attention
papers.authors: Schneider, D., Oezdemir, S., Wascher, E., Arnau, S.
papers.evidence: 自发性眨眼的时间分布作为内部注意的时间标记，与眼动追踪和意图识别相关
papers.tldr: 自发性眨眼是脑电中最大的生理伪迹，通常被移除。但研究发现眨眼的时机可能携带认知信息。本研究通过两个实验探究眨眼的时间分布能否作为工作记忆内部注意聚焦的时间解析行为指标。实验1中，眨眼锁定EEG分析显示眨眼时机与注意聚焦的神经活动对齐。实验2中，根据线索时机调整眨眼频率分布。眨眼与记忆表现相关，表明自发眨眼可作为追踪潜在认知处理的无干扰计时信号。
papers.abstract_en: Eye blinks are among the largest physiological artefacts in electroencephalography and are typically removed from the recordings. Yet their timing may carry information about cognition. Here, we asked whether the temporal distribution of spontaneous blinks across trials provides a time-resolved behavioural signature of internal attentional focusing in working memory. In Experiment 1, blink-locked EEG analyses showed that blink timing was aligned with neural activity reflecting attentional focusing on a relevant internal representation. In Experiment 2, participants remembered the same visual information across conditions, but the relevant item was cued either early during storage, or later with presentation of the memory probe. Blink-frequency profiles shifted accordingly, increasing after the cue when selection was possible early and after the probe when selection was delayed. Post-cue blinks in the early-selection condition were also associated with better memory performance. Thus, more generally, spontaneous blinks might provide an unobtrusive chronometric signal for tracking latent cognitive processing.

papers.title_en: Distinct Computational and Temporal Mechanisms Underlie the Joint Effects of Motivation and Working Memory on Perceptual Sensitivity
papers.evidence: 使用瞳孔测量研究动机和工作记忆对感知的影响，涉及理解人类目标的计算模型
papers.tldr: 人类知觉受内部认知状态调节，但动机与工作记忆如何联合影响知觉敏感性尚不明确。本研究结合行为实验、贝叶斯心理测量模型和瞳孔测量，系统操控动机和工作记忆状态。结果发现两者独立加性贡献于知觉敏感性，瞳孔动态反映其时间分离影响。该发现揭示了动机和工作记忆的不同计算和时间机制，为自上而下知觉调控提供统一框架。
papers.abstract_en: Human perception is continuously shaped by internal cognitive states, yet how motivation and working memory jointly influence perceptual sensitivity remains poorly understood. Here, we combined behavioural experiments, computational modelling, and pupillometry to determine whether motivation enhances perception by amplifying working memory (WM)-driven facilitation or whether both exert independent influences. Participants performed a near-threshold visuospatial discrimination task under systematically manipulated motivational and WM states. Behaviourally, both motivation and WM independently improved perceptual performance, producing the greatest enhancement when both were present. A Bayesian generalized linear psychometric model revealed that these improvements were best explained by independent additive contributions to effective perceptual sensitivity, rather than motivational amplification of WM. Consistent with this computational framework, pupil dynamics tracked trial-by-trial fluctuations in effective perceptual sensitivity while revealing temporally dissociable influences of WM and motivation during perceptual decision making. Together, our findings demonstrate that the joint effects of motivation and working memory arise from distinct computational and temporal mechanisms, providing a unified framework for understanding top-down regulation of human perception.
